### 2. Database Models (SQLAlchemy ORM)

**User** (`backend/models/user.py`):
| Column | Type | Default |
|--------|------|---------|
| id | Integer PK | autoincrement |
| email | String(256) UNIQUE | required |
| password_hash | String(256) | required |
| role | String(32) | "admin" |
| created_at | DateTime | utcnow |
| subscription_status | String(32) | "free" |
| plan_tier | String(32) | "free" |
| stripe_customer_id | String(256) | nullable |
| stripe_subscription_id | String(256) | nullable |
| subscription_expires_at | DateTime | nullable |
| monthly_predictions_used | Integer | 0 |
| predictions_month_reset | DateTime | nullable |

**Client** (`backend/models/client.py`): id, name, email, phone, wilaya, city, created_at
**Prediction** (`backend/models/prediction.py`): id, user_id, client_id, input_json (JSON), prediction (String), probability (Float), decision (String), explanation (JSON), created_at
**Log** (`backend/models/log.py`): id, action, level, details (JSON), created_at
**ModelVersion** (`backend/models/model_version.py`): id, version, metrics (JSON), created_at

### 3. Authentication System

- **JWT-based** with access tokens (1 hour expiry) and refresh tokens (7 days expiry)
- Tokens encode subject (email) and scope (access/refresh) using HS256
- Password hashing via **bcrypt**
- **Two roles**: `admin` and `client`
- Role is stored in the `User.role` column (default: "admin")
- Frontend stores tokens in `localStorage` + cookies (`paypredict.token`, `paypredict.refresh_token`, `paypredict.session`)
- **AuthContext** (`frontend/contexts/auth-context.tsx`) manages login/logout/refresh flow
- **AuthGuard** wraps dashboard routes; **requireAdmin**/**requireClient**/**requireAuth** guards from `lib/auth-guards.ts`
- Default admin: `admin@example.com` / `admin1234`

### 4. All API Endpoints (Flask Backend)

**Auth Routes** (`/auth/*`) - `backend/auth/blueprints.py`:
| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
|--------|------|------|-------------|
| POST | /auth/register | Public | Register new client user |
| POST | /auth/login | Public | Login, returns JWT tokens + user info |
| POST | /auth/refresh | Bearer Token (refresh) | Refresh access token |
| GET | /auth/me | Bearer Token | Get current user info |
| POST | /auth/logout | Bearer Token | Logout |
| GET | /auth/admins | Admin | List all admins |
| POST | /auth/admins | Admin | Create new admin |
| GET | /auth/users | Admin | List all users |
| PUT | /auth/users/:id/role | Admin | Update user role (admin/client) |
| PUT | /auth/users/:id/plan | Admin | Update user plan (free/pro/enterprise/unlimited) |
| DELETE | /auth/users/:id | Admin | Delete user |

**API Routes** (`/api/*`) - `backend/api/blueprints.py`:
| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
|--------|------|------|-------------|
| GET | /api/clients | Bearer Token | List all clients |
| POST | /api/clients | Bearer Token | Create new client |
| DELETE | /api/clients/:id | Bearer Token | Delete client |
| POST | /api/predict | Bearer Token | Single prediction (requires client_id) |
| GET | /api/history | Bearer Token | Prediction history for current user |
| GET | /api/analytics | Bearer Token | Analytics (total, avg prob, risk dist, monthly) |
| GET | /api/usage | Bearer Token | Usage stats (plan_tier, used, limit, remaining) |
| GET | /api/features | Bearer Token | ML model feature names |
| GET | /api/health | Public | Health check + model loaded status |

**Payment Routes** (`/payment/*`) - `backend/api/payment_routes.py`:
| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
|--------|------|------|-------------|
| POST | /payment/create-checkout-session | Bearer Token | Create Stripe checkout (pro/enterprise) |
| POST | /payment/webhook/stripe | Public (sig verified) | Stripe webhook handler |
| GET | /payment/subscription-status | Bearer Token | Get subscription status |

**Chat Routes** - `backend/api/chat_routes.py`:
| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
|--------|------|------|-------------|
| POST | /chat | Optional | AI chatbot (OpenAI/Claude/Mock), supports en/fr/ar |

### 5. Frontend Pages/Routes (Next.js App Router)

| Path | Component | Auth Required | Description |
|------|-----------|---------------|-------------|
| / | page.tsx | No | Landing page (Hero, Features, Pricing, etc.) |
| /login | login/page.tsx | No | Login form with JWT auth |
| /dashboard | dashboard/page.tsx | Yes | Dashboard overview (Admin or Client) |
| /dashboard/admins | dashboard/admins/page.tsx | Yes | Admin management (list, create, search) |
| /dashboard/analytics | dashboard/analytics/page.tsx | Yes | Analytics charts and reports |
| /dashboard/billing | dashboard/billing/page.tsx | Yes | Billing/subscription management |
| /dashboard/clients | dashboard/clients/page.tsx | Yes | Client management |
| /dashboard/help | dashboard/help/page.tsx | Yes | Help/support |
| /dashboard/notifications | dashboard/notifications/page.tsx | Yes | Notifications |
| /dashboard/predict | dashboard/predict/page.tsx | Yes | Single prediction form |
| /dashboard/profile | dashboard/profile/page.tsx | Yes | User profile |
| /dashboard/reports | dashboard/reports/page.tsx | Yes | Reports |
| /dashboard/settings | dashboard/settings/page.tsx | Yes | Settings |
| /dashboard/subscriptions | dashboard/subscriptions/page.tsx | Yes | Manage subscriptions |
| /dashboard/upload | dashboard/upload/page.tsx | Yes | CSV batch upload for predictions |
| /dashboard/users | dashboard/users/page.tsx | Yes | User management (admin) |
| /about | about/page.tsx | No | About page |
| /analytics | analytics/page.tsx | No | Public analytics |
| /billing | billing/page.tsx | No | Pricing/plans |
| /clients | clients/page.tsx | No | Client info |
| /management | management/page.tsx | No | Management info |
| /prediction | prediction/page.tsx | No | Prediction info |
| /profile | profile/page.tsx | No | Public profile |
| /subscriptions | subscriptions/page.tsx | No | Subscription plans |
| /upload | upload/page.tsx | No | Upload info |

### 6. Supabase/PostgreSQL Configuration

- **Optional** Supabase integration via `SUPABASE_URL` and `SUPABASE_KEY` in `.env`
- `DatabaseService` attempts to create a Supabase client (in `services/db.py`) 
- When active, predictions, logs, and users are mirrored to Supabase tables
- The primary database is **SQLite** (`credit_risk.db`) via SQLAlchemy
- Configuration supports switching to PostgreSQL via `DATABASE_URL` env var

### 7. Key Dependencies (from node_modules)

**Frontend**: Next.js 15+, React, TailwindCSS, Framer Motion, shadcn/ui components, recharts, react-hook-form, next-intl, sonner (toasts), vaul (drawer), next-themes, lucide-react, zod
**Backend**: Flask, Flask-CORS, Flask-JWT-Extended, Flask-Limiter, SQLAlchemy, Supabase, bcrypt, PyJWT, Stripe, Redis, XGBoost, scikit-learn, pandas, joblib, SHAP, gunicorn

### 8. Admin Pages/Components

- **`/dashboard`** - Shows AdminDashboard or ClientDashboard based on user role
- **`/dashboard/admins`** - Full admin CRUD (list, search, create with password)
- **`/dashboard/users`** - User management page
- **`/dashboard/analytics`** - Analytics with risk distribution and monthly trends
- **`/dashboard/clients`** - Client management
- **AdminDashboard component** - KPI cards (total users, predictions, high risk cases, system health), user list, quick actions, recent activity
- **Admin API endpoints**: List admins, create admin, list users, update role, update plan, delete user

### 9. Current User Model Roles

The User model has a `role` column supporting exactly two values:
- **`admin`** - Full access, can manage users, view all data, create other admins
- **`client`** - Limited access, can make predictions, view own history, manage own clients

Plan tiers: `free` (100 preds/month limit), `pro`, `enterprise`, `unlimited`

### 10. i18n Support

- Three languages: English (`en`), French (`fr`), Arabic (`ar`)
- Using `next-intl` with JSON message files in `/messages/`
- `LanguageContext` provides `t()`, `locale`, `isRTL` throughout the app
- Chatbot responses also support all three languages

Next steps:
The project is fully mapped. Next steps could include: reviewing specific page implementations for admin features, checking Stripe configuration, examining CSV upload handling, or reviewing the ML model training pipeline.

| /prediction | prediction/page.tsx | No | Prediction info |
| /profile | profile/page.tsx | No | Public profile |
| /subscriptions | subscriptions/page.tsx | No | Subscription plans |
| /upload | upload/page.tsx | No | Upload info |

### 6. Supabase/PostgreSQL Configuration

- **Optional** Supabase integration via `SUPABASE_URL` and `SUPABASE_KEY` in `.env`
- `DatabaseService` attempts to create a Supabase client (in `services/db.py`) 
- When active, predictions, logs, and users are mirrored to Supabase tables
- The primary database is **SQLite** (`credit_risk.db`) via SQLAlchemy
- Configuration supports switching to PostgreSQL via `DATABASE_URL` env var

### 7. Key Dependencies (from node_modules)

**Frontend**: Next.js 15+, React, TailwindCSS, Framer Motion, shadcn/ui components, recharts, react-hook-form, next-intl, sonner (toasts), vaul (drawer), next-themes, lucide-react, zod
**Backend**: Flask, Flask-CORS, Flask-JWT-Extended, Flask-Limiter, SQLAlchemy, Supabase, bcrypt, PyJWT, Stripe, Redis, XGBoost, scikit-learn, pandas, joblib, SHAP, gunicorn

### 8. Admin Pages/Components

- **`/dashboard`** - Shows AdminDashboard or ClientDashboard based on user role
- **`/dashboard/admins`** - Full admin CRUD (list, search, create with password)
- **`/dashboard/users`** - User management page
- **`/dashboard/analytics`** - Analytics with risk distribution and monthly trends
- **`/dashboard/clients`** - Client management
- **AdminDashboard component** - KPI cards (total users, predictions, high risk cases, system health), user list, quick actions, recent activity
- **Admin API endpoints**: List admins, create admin, list users, update role, update plan, delete user

### 9. Current User Model Roles

The User model has a `role` column supporting exactly two values:
- **`admin`** - Full access, can manage users, view all data, create other admins
- **`client`** - Limited access, can make predictions, view own history, manage own clients

Plan tiers: `free` (100 preds/month limit), `pro`, `enterprise`, `unlimited`

### 10. i18n Support

- Three languages: English (`en`), French (`fr`), Arabic (`ar`)
- Using `next-intl` with JSON message files in `/messages/`
- `LanguageContext` provides `t()`, `locale`, `isRTL` throughout the app
- Chatbot responses also support all three languages

### 2. Database Models (SQLAlchemy ORM)

**User** (`backend/models/user.py`):
| Column | Type | Default |
|--------|------|---------|
| id | Integer PK | autoincrement |
| email | String(256) UNIQUE | required |
| password_hash | String(256) | required |
| role | String(32) | "admin" |
| created_at | DateTime | utcnow |
| subscription_status | String(32) | "free" |
| plan_tier | String(32) | "free" |
| stripe_customer_id | String(256) | nullable |
| stripe_subscription_id | String(256) | nullable |
| subscription_expires_at | DateTime | nullable |
| monthly_predictions_used | Integer | 0 |
| predictions_month_reset | DateTime | nullable |

**Client** (`backend/models/client.py`): id, name, email, phone, wilaya, city, created_at
**Prediction** (`backend/models/prediction.py`): id, user_id, client_id, input_json (JSON), prediction (String), probability (Float), decision (String), explanation (JSON), created_at
**Log** (`backend/models/log.py`): id, action, level, details (JSON), created_at
**ModelVersion** (`backend/models/model_version.py`): id, version, metrics (JSON), created_at

### 3. Authentication System

- **JWT-based** with access tokens (1 hour expiry) and refresh tokens (7 days expiry)
- Tokens encode subject (email) and scope (access/refresh) using HS256
- Password hashing via **bcrypt**
- **Two roles**: `admin` and `client`
- Role is stored in the `User.role` column (default: "admin")
- Frontend stores tokens in `localStorage` + cookies (`paypredict.token`, `paypredict.refresh_token`, `paypredict.session`)
- **AuthContext** (`frontend/contexts/auth-context.tsx`) manages login/logout/refresh flow
- **AuthGuard** wraps dashboard routes; **requireAdmin**/**requireClient**/**requireAuth** guards from `lib/auth-guards.ts`
- Default admin: `admin@example.com` / `admin1234`

### 4. All API Endpoints (Flask Backend)

**Auth Routes** (`/auth/*`) - `backend/auth/blueprints.py`:
| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
|--------|------|------|-------------|
| POST | /auth/register | Public | Register new client user |
| POST | /auth/login | Public | Login, returns JWT tokens + user info |
| POST | /auth/refresh | Bearer Token (refresh) | Refresh access token |
| GET | /auth/me | Bearer Token | Get current user info |
| POST | /auth/logout | Bearer Token | Logout |
| GET | /auth/admins | Admin | List all admins |
| POST | /auth/admins | Admin | Create new admin |
| GET | /auth/users | Admin | List all users |
| PUT | /auth/users/:id/role | Admin | Update user role (admin/client) |
| PUT | /auth/users/:id/plan | Admin | Update user plan (free/pro/enterprise/unlimited) |
| DELETE | /auth/users/:id | Admin | Delete user |

**API Routes** (`/api/*`) - `backend/api/blueprints.py`:
| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
|--------|------|------|-------------|
| GET | /api/clients | Bearer Token | List all clients |
| POST | /api/clients | Bearer Token | Create new client |
| DELETE | /api/clients/:id | Bearer Token | Delete client |
| POST | /api/predict | Bearer Token | Single prediction (requires client_id) |
| GET | /api/history | Bearer Token | Prediction history for current user |
| GET | /api/analytics | Bearer Token | Analytics (total, avg prob, risk dist, monthly) |
| GET | /api/usage | Bearer Token | Usage stats (plan_tier, used, limit, remaining) |
| GET | /api/features | Bearer Token | ML model feature names |
| GET | /api/health | Public | Health check + model loaded status |

**Payment Routes** (`/payment/*`) - `backend/api/payment_routes.py`:
| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
|--------|------|------|-------------|
| POST | /payment/create-checkout-session | Bearer Token | Create Stripe checkout (pro/enterprise) |
| POST | /payment/webhook/stripe | Public (sig verified) | Stripe webhook handler |
| GET | /payment/subscription-status | Bearer Token | Get subscription status |

**Chat Routes** - `backend/api/chat_routes.py`:
| Method | Path | Auth | Description |
|--------|------|------|-------------|
| POST | /chat | Optional | AI chatbot (OpenAI/Claude/Mock), supports en/fr/ar |

### 5. Frontend Pages/Routes (Next.js App Router)

| Path | Component | Auth Required | Description |

toolCalls.description:
Launch a new agent to handle complex, multi-step tasks autonomously.

The Task tool launches specialized agents that autonomously handle complex tasks. Each agent has specific capabilities and tools available to it.

Available agents (use these names as the subagent_name value):
- explore: Fast read-only codebase search agent for finding files, patterns, and answering questions about the codebase. Use when you need to quickly find files by patterns, search code for keywords, or understand how parts of the codebase work. Specify thoroughness: 'quick' for basic searches, 'medium' for moderate exploration, 'very thorough' for comprehensive analysis. (Tools: All tools except replace, write_file, apply_patch, save_memory)
- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)
- plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except replace, write_file, apply_patch, save_memory)

Agent selection guidance:
- For codebase search, file discovery, or answering "where is X" questions, prefer the explore agent instead of general-purpose — it is faster and read-only.
- Use explore for quick searches, code pattern discovery, and understanding how parts of the codebase work.
- Use the plan agent for designing implementation strategies before writing code — it explores the codebase and returns a step-by-step plan with critical files.
- Prefer plan over general-purpose when the user asks to design, architect, or plan before implementing.
- Use general-purpose for multi-step tasks that require writing code, running commands, or performing actions beyond read-only exploration.

When using the Task tool, you must set the subagent_name parameter to one of the agent names listed above (e.g., "general-purpose").

When NOT to use the Task tool:
- If you want to read a specific file path, use the read_file or glob tool instead of the Task tool, to find the match more quickly
- If you are searching for a specific class definition like "class Foo", use the glob tool instead, to find the match more quickly
- If you are searching for code within a specific file or set of 2-3 files, use the read_file tool instead of the Task tool, to find the match more quickly
- Other tasks that are not related to the agent descriptions above

Usage notes:
- Always include a short description (3-5 words) summarizing what the agent will do
- Launch multiple agents concurrently whenever possible, to maximize performance; to do that, use a single message with multiple tool uses
- When the agent is done, it will return a single message back to you. The result returned by the agent is not visible to the user. To show the user the result, you should send a text message back to the user with a concise summary of the result.
- Optional display name: the name parameter is only for UI/tracking display. If omitted, description is used as the display name.
- You can optionally run agents in the background using the run_in_background parameter, but only for genuinely independent work. When an agent runs in the background, you will be notified when it completes.
- Foreground is the default. Use foreground when you need the agent's results before deciding the next step, especially research that informs implementation.
- Use background only when the work is genuinely independent and can safely complete later, such as long-running tests, build validation, independent audits, or non-overlapping research.
- After launching background tasks, do NOT call `task_output` to poll or block — continue with non-overlapping work or respond to the user. Only check progress if the user explicitly asks or the result was truncated.
- Never guess or summarize background task results before the `<bg_task_done ...>...</bg_task_done>` notification arrives.
- Do not duplicate work on the same files or topic as a running background task.
- If this execution corresponds to an existing planned job, pass job_id so the linked job is auto-claimed and auto-updated on completion/failure.
- IMPORTANT: After launching background tasks, do NOT call `task_output` to poll or block — continue with other work or respond to the user. When a background task finishes, a `<bg_task_done task_id="..." name="..." status="...">result</bg_task_done>` message is automatically injected into the conversation containing the task result. Use this directly, and only use `task_output` if the result was truncated or you need additional details.
- Provide clear, detailed prompts so the agent can work autonomously and return exactly the information you need.
- The agent's outputs should generally be trusted
- Clearly tell the agent whether you expect it to write code or just to do research (search, file reads, web fetches, etc.), since it is not aware of the user's intent
- If the agent description mentions that it should be used proactively, then you should try your best to use it without the user having to ask for it first. Use your judgement.
- If the user specifies that they want you to run agents "in parallel", you MUST send a single message with multiple Task tool use content blocks.

Example usage:
<example>
user: "Please refactor the authentication module to use JWT"
assistant: First let me explore the existing auth implementation.
assistant: Uses the Task tool to launch a general-purpose agent to search for and understand the current authentication code
assistant: Based on the exploration results, I'll now implement the JWT refactoring.
</example>
<example>
user: "Where is the database connection configured?"
assistant: Uses the Task tool to launch a general-purpose agent to find database configuration files
</example>

toolCalls.description:
Reads content from multiple files specified by paths or glob patterns within a configured target directory. For text files, it concatenates their content into a single string. It is primarily designed for text-based files. However, it can also process image (e.g., .png, .jpg) and PDF (.pdf) files if their file names or extensions are explicitly included in the 'paths' argument. For these explicitly requested non-text files, their data is read and included in a format suitable for model consumption (e.g., base64 encoded).

This tool is useful when you need to understand or analyze a collection of files, such as:
- Getting an overview of a codebase or parts of it (e.g., all TypeScript files in the 'src' directory).
- Finding where specific functionality is implemented if the user asks broad questions about code.
- Reviewing documentation files (e.g., all Markdown files in the 'docs' directory).
- Gathering context from multiple configuration files.
- When the user asks to "read all files in X directory" or "show me the content of all Y files".

Use this tool when the user's query implies needing the content of several files simultaneously for context, analysis, or summarization. For text files, it uses default UTF-8 encoding and a '--- {filePath} ---' separator between file contents. Ensure paths are relative to the target directory. Glob patterns like 'src/**/*.js' are supported. Avoid using for single files if a more specific single-file reading tool is available, unless the user specifically requests to process a list containing just one file via this tool. Other binary files (not explicitly requested as image/PDF) are generally skipped. Default excludes apply to common non-text files (except for explicitly requested images/PDFs) and large dependency directories unless 'useDefaultExcludes' is false.

args.content:
# PayPredict — SaaS Admin & Production-Ready Plan

## Vue d'ensemble

Le projet a déjà une base solide : auth JWT, rôles admin/client, dashboard basique, Flask backend avec SQLAlchemy. L'objectif est de **transformer ce MVP en SaaS production-ready** avec un vrai dashboard admin, un système de plans/abonnements, et une UX professionnelle — **sans rien casser**.

## Analyse de l'existant

### Ce qui fonctionne déjà :
- Auth JWT avec rôles admin/client ✅
- Login détecte le rôle et redirige vers admin/client dashboard ✅
- Backend Flask avec endpoints CRUD users/admins/clients ✅
- Modèle User avec `role`, `plan_tier`, `subscription_status` ✅
- Frontend Next.js 15 + shadcn/ui + recharts + framer-motion ✅
- Sidebar + Topbar + AuthGuard ✅
- Pages dashboard/admins, dashboard/users, dashboard/clients ✅
- Pages subscriptions, billing, analytics ✅
- i18n (en/fr/ar) ✅
- Dark/light mode ✅

### Ce qui manque / est cassé :
1. **Admin Dashboard** — utilise des données factices (stats placeholder, "recent activity" vide, fake trends)
2. **Backend** — Pas d'endpoint admin pour stats globales, pas de middleware rôle sur `/api/`, pas de logs endpoint
3. **Users page** — charge seulement les admins (via `listAdmins`), pas tous les users
4. **Billing page** — données 100% factices (revenueData hardcoded)
5. **Free plan** — limite à 100 prédictions au lieu de 2-3 comme demandé
6. **Plans** — pas de distinction monthly vs yearly réelle, pas de DA pricing cohérent
7. **Profile** — données hardcoded ("John Davis"), pas connecté à l'utilisateur réel
8. **Dashboard page.tsx** — a du code dupliqué/cassé après le fallback (syntax error JSX)
9. **Sidebar** — montre les mêmes liens pour admin et client (pas de filtrage par rôle)
10. **Aucune route backend** pour : admin stats, activation/désactivation compte, logs système, secteurs
11. **Aucun middleware rôle** sur les routes `/api/` — n'importe quel user authentifié peut tout voir

---

## Plan d'implémentation (8 phases)

### Phase 1 : Backend — Admin API & Middleware Rôles
**Fichiers modifiés :**
- `backend/api/blueprints.py` — Ajouter `require_admin()` decorator, nouveaux endpoints admin
- `backend/services/db.py` — Nouvelles méthodes : `get_admin_stats()`, `toggle_user_active()`, `get_system_logs()`, `get_all_predictions()`

**Nouveaux endpoints :**
| Méthode | Path | Rôle | Description |
|---------|------|------|-------------|
| GET | `/api/admin/stats` | admin | Stats globales (users, predictions, revenue, subscriptions) |
| GET | `/api/admin/predictions` | admin | Toutes les prédictions (tous users) |
| PUT | `/api/admin/users/:id/toggle-active` | admin | Activer/désactiver un compte |
| GET | `/api/admin/logs` | admin | Logs système |
| GET | `/api/admin/revenue` | admin | Données revenus |

**Modèle User — Ajouter champ `is_active` :**
- `backend/models/user.py` — Ajouter `is_active = Column(Boolean, default=True)`
- `backend/services/db.py` — Respecter `is_active` dans `get_user_by_email()`
- `backend/auth/blueprints.py` — Vérifier `is_active` au login

**Middleware rôle sur `/api/` :**
- Ajouter `require_admin()` dans blueprints.py pour les routes admin
- Sécuriser `/api/history` et `/api/analytics` pour ne retourner que les données du user (si client)

---

### Phase 2 : Free Plan — 3 prédictions gratuites
**Fichiers modifiés :**
- `backend/services/db.py` — Changer `check_prediction_limit()` : free = 3 prédictions (pas 100)
- `frontend/app/dashboard/subscriptions/page.tsx` — Mettre à jour les plans

**Nouveaux plans en DA :**
| Plan | Prix | Prédictions | Features |
|------|------|-------------|----------|
| Free | 0 DA | 3 total (reset jamais sauf upgrade) | Single prediction, basic analytics |
| Pro Monthly | 2 500 DA/mois | Illimité | Batch, API, advanced analytics |
| Pro Yearly | 24 000 DA/an (20% réduction) | Illimité | Même + badge, priorité |
| Enterprise | Sur devis | Illimité | Tout + support dédié |

**Frontend — Popup upgrade :**
- Quand un client free atteint sa limite → modal/badge visible qui pousse vers l'upgrade
- `frontend/app/dashboard/client-dashboard.tsx` — Afficher upgrade prompt quand limit atteinte

---

### Phase 3 : Admin Dashboard — Vrai dashboard avec vraies données
**Fichiers modifiés :**
- `frontend/app/dashboard/admin-dashboard.tsx` — Réécrire avec données réelles de `/api/admin/stats`
- `frontend/lib/api.ts` — Ajouter méthodes API admin

**Nouvelles méthodes API frontend :**
```typescript
adminStats: () => request<AdminStats>("/api/admin/stats")
adminAllPredictions: () => request<{predictions: Prediction[]}>("/api/admin/predictions")
adminToggleUser: (id: number) => request<{ok: boolean}>(`/api/admin/users/${id}/toggle-active`, {method: "PUT"})
adminLogs: () => request<{logs: Log[]}>("/api/admin/logs")
listUsers: () => request<{users: ApiUser[]}>("/auth/users")
deleteUser: (id: number) => request<{ok: boolean}>(`/auth/users/${id}`, {method: "DELETE"})
```

**Admin Dashboard sections :**
1. KPI Cards (total users, total clients, total predictions, active subscriptions, revenue, high risk)
2. Charts (recharts) — Predictions trend, risk distribution, revenue trend, sector usage
3. Users table avec recherche, filtres, pagination, toggle active, delete
4. Recent predictions table (tous users)
5. System logs section
6. Quick actions

**Design** : Style Stripe/Vercel/Linear — glassmorphism léger, animations framer-motion, couleurs cohérentes (#164A41, #F1B24A, #4D774E)

---

### Phase 4 : Client Dashboard — Données privées par utilisateur
**Fichiers modifiés :**
- `backend/api/blueprints.py` — Sécuriser `/api/history` pour ne retourner que les prédictions du user connecté
- `backend/api/blueprints.py` — Sécuriser `/api/analytics` pour ne retourner que les analytics du user
- `frontend/app/dashboard/client-dashboard.tsx` — Améliorer avec vraies données

**Isolation des données :**
- `/api/history` — Si user est client, filtrer par `user_id` uniquement
- `/api/analytics` — Si user est client, analytics que pour ses prédictions
- `/api/clients` — Si user est client, que SES clients (nécessite `user_id` sur Client model)

**Ajouter `owner_id` au modèle Client :**
- `backend/models/client.py` — Ajouter `owner_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey('users.id'), nullable=True)`
- Migration SQLite automatique via `_migrate_sqlite()`
- `backend/services/db.py` — Filtrer par `owner_id` pour les clients

---

### Phase 5 : Users Management Page — Vrai CRUD
**Fichiers modifiés :**
- `frontend/app/dashboard/users/page.tsx` — Réécrire avec `listUsers()` (pas `listAdmins()`)
- `frontend/lib/api.ts` — Ajouter `listUsers()`, `deleteUser()`, `updateUserRole()`, `updateUserPlan()`

**Features :**
- Table complète avec tous les users (admins + clients)
- Recherche, filtre par rôle/plan
- Toggle active/inactive
- Changer rôle
- Changer plan
- Supprimer
- Pagination

---

### Phase 6 : Profile Page — Connecté au vrai utilisateur
**Fichiers modifiés :**
- `frontend/app/dashboard/profile/page.tsx` — Utiliser les vraies données de `useAuth().user`
- Retirer "John Davis" et les données factices

---

### Phase 7 : Sidebar adaptatif par rôle + Fixes
**Fichiers modifiés :**
- `frontend/components/dashboard/sidebar.tsx` — Filtrer les liens par rôle
  - Admin : Dashboard, Predict, Upload, Analytics, Clients, Users, Admins, Billing, Subscriptions, Settings
  - Client : Dashboard, Predict, Upload, Analytics, Clients, Subscriptions, Profile, Settings
- `frontend/app/dashboard/page.tsx` — Corriger le code dupliqué/cassé (syntax error JSX après le fallback)
- `frontend/app/dashboard/billing/page.tsx` — Remplacer données factices par vraies données API

---

### Phase 8 : UX/UI Polish & Vérifications finales
**Fichiers modifiés :**
- Animations / transitions (déjà framer-motion présent)
- Skeleton loaders pour les pages de chargement
- Responsive mobile/tablette (vérifier les tables)
- Corriger erreurs TypeScript
- Corriger erreurs console
- Vérifier tous les boutons fonctionnels
- Vérifier les liens

**Vérifications :**
- `npm run build` sans erreurs
- Backend Flask démarre sans erreurs
- Login fonctionne (admin → dashboard admin, client → dashboard client)
- Predictions fonctionnent
- Upload CSV fonctionne
- Toutes les pages dashboard sont accessibles
- Pas de données factices visibles
- Pas de boutons fake
- Pas de liens cassés

---

## Ordre d'implémentation

1. Phase 1 (Backend) — Fondation nécessaire pour tout le reste
2. Phase 2 (Free plan 3 preds) — Changement rapide et important
3. Phase 3 (Admin Dashboard) — Feature principale demandée
4. Phase 4 (Client data isolation) — Sécurité critique
5. Phase 5 (Users page) — Dépend du backend Phase 1
6. Phase 6 (Profile) — Quick fix
7. Phase 7 (Sidebar + fixes) — Polish
8. Phase 8 (UX/UI + verification) — Final

## Risques et mitigations

- **Migration SQLite** : Ajout de colonnes — géré par `_migrate_sqlite()` existant + `create_all()`
- **Breaking changes API** : Les nouveaux endpoints sont sous `/api/admin/` — pas de conflit
- **Données existantes** : Le `is_active` par défaut à `True` ne casse rien
- **Profile factice** : Remplacement par données réelles — pas de regression

messages.content:
Voici le plan structuré en **8 phases** :

| Phase | Description | Impact |
|-------|-------------|--------|
| **1** | Backend — Admin API, middleware rôles, `is_active` sur User, nouveaux endpoints admin | Critique |
| **2** | Free plan → 3 prédictions gratuites (au lieu de 100), plans en DA | Demandé |
| **3** | Admin Dashboard — Vrai dashboard avec données réelles, charts, tables | Principal |
| **4** | Isolation données client — chaque client voit seulement SES données | Sécurité |
| **5** | Users page — CRUD complet (list all, toggle active, delete, change role/plan) | Admin |
| **6** | Profile page — Connecté au vrai utilisateur (plus de "John Davis") | Fix |
| **7** | Sidebar adaptatif par rôle + fix page.tsx cassé + billing données réelles | Polish |
| **8** | UX/UI polish, skeleton loaders, vérifications TypeScript/build | Final |

**Points clés du plan :**
- Aucun composant existant supprimé — uniquement des améliorations et ajouts
- Backend : 5 nouveaux endpoints admin + middleware rôle + champ `is_active`
- Frontend : Admin dashboard réécrit avec vraies données recharts, users page avec CRUD complet
- Plans : Free (3 preds), Pro Monthly (2 500 DA), Pro Yearly (24 000 DA, -20%), Enterprise (sur devis)
- Style conservé : palette #164A41/#F1B24A/#4D774E, shadcn/ui, framer-motion

Use this tool to create a structured task list for your current coding session. This helps you track progress, organize complex tasks, and demonstrate thoroughness to the user.
It also helps the user understand the progress of the job and overall progress of their requests.

## When to Use This Tool
Use this tool proactively in these scenarios:
- Complex multi-step tasks - When a job requires 3 or more distinct steps or actions
- Non-trivial and complex tasks - Work that requires careful planning or multiple operations
- Plan mode - When using plan mode, create a job list to track the work
- User explicitly requests job list - When the user directly asks you to use the job list
- User provides multiple tasks - When users provide a list of things to be done (numbered or comma-separated)
- After receiving new instructions - Immediately capture user requirements as jobs
- When you start working on a job - Mark it as in_progress BEFORE beginning work
- After completing a job - Mark it as completed and add any new follow-up jobs discovered during implementation

## When NOT to Use This Tool
Skip using this tool when:
- There is only a single, straightforward job
- The job is trivial and tracking it provides no organizational benefit
- The job can be completed in less than 3 trivial steps
- The job is purely conversational or informational

NOTE that you should not use this tool if there is only one trivial job to do. In this case you are better off just doing the job directly.

## Job Fields
- **subject**: A brief, actionable title in imperative form (e.g., "Fix authentication bug in login flow")
- **description**: Detailed description of what needs to be done, including context and acceptance criteria
- **activeForm**: Present continuous form shown in spinner when job is in_progress (e.g., "Fixing authentication bug"). This is displayed to the user while you work on the job.

**IMPORTANT**: Always provide activeForm when creating jobs. The subject should be imperative ("Run tests") while activeForm should be present continuous ("Running tests"). All jobs are created with status `pending`.

## Tips
- Create jobs with clear, specific subjects that describe the outcome
- Include enough detail in the description for another agent to understand and complete the job
- After creating jobs, use job_update to set up dependencies (blocks/blockedBy) if needed
- Check job_list first to avoid creating duplicate jobs
- Use `allowDuplicate: true` only when you intentionally need a second open job with the same normalized subject

Use this tool to create a structured task list for your current coding session. This helps you track progress, organize complex tasks, and demonstrate thoroughness to the user.
It also helps the user understand the progress of the job and overall progress of their requests.

## When to Use This Tool
Use this tool proactively in these scenarios:
- Complex multi-step tasks - When a job requires 3 or more distinct steps or actions
- Non-trivial and complex tasks - Work that requires careful planning or multiple operations
- Plan mode - When using plan mode, create a job list to track the work
- User explicitly requests job list - When the user directly asks you to use the job list
- User provides multiple tasks - When users provide a list of things to be done (numbered or comma-separated)
- After receiving new instructions - Immediately capture user requirements as jobs
- When you start working on a job - Mark it as in_progress BEFORE beginning work
- After completing a job - Mark it as completed and add any new follow-up jobs discovered during implementation

## When NOT to Use This Tool
Skip using this tool when:
- There is only a single, straightforward job
- The job is trivial and tracking it provides no organizational benefit
- The job can be completed in less than 3 trivial steps
- The job is purely conversational or informational

NOTE that you should not use this tool if there is only one trivial job to do. In this case you are better off just doing the job directly.

## Job Fields
- **subject**: A brief, actionable title in imperative form (e.g., "Fix authentication bug in login flow")
- **description**: Detailed description of what needs to be done, including context and acceptance criteria
- **activeForm**: Present continuous form shown in spinner when job is in_progress (e.g., "Fixing authentication bug"). This is displayed to the user while you work on the job.

**IMPORTANT**: Always provide activeForm when creating jobs. The subject should be imperative ("Run tests") while activeForm should be present continuous ("Running tests"). All jobs are created with status `pending`.

## Tips
- Create jobs with clear, specific subjects that describe the outcome
- Include enough detail in the description for another agent to understand and complete the job
- After creating jobs, use job_update to set up dependencies (blocks/blockedBy) if needed
- Check job_list first to avoid creating duplicate jobs
- Use `allowDuplicate: true` only when you intentionally need a second open job with the same normalized subject

toolCalls.description:
Use this tool to update a job in the job list.

## When to Use This Tool
**Mark jobs as resolved:**
- When you have completed the work described in a job
- When a job is no longer needed or has been superseded
- IMPORTANT: Always mark your assigned jobs as resolved when you finish them
- After resolving, call job_list to find your next job
- ONLY mark a job as completed when you have FULLY accomplished it
- If you encounter errors, blockers, or cannot finish, keep the job as in_progress
- When blocked, create a new job describing what needs to be resolved
- Never mark a job as completed if:
  - Tests are failing
  - Implementation is partial
  - You encountered unresolved errors
  - You couldn't find necessary files or dependencies

**Delete jobs:**
- When a job is no longer relevant or was created in error
- Setting status to `deleted` permanently removes the job

**Update job details:**
- When requirements change or become clearer
- When establishing dependencies between jobs

## Fields You Can Update
- **status**: The job status (see Status Workflow below)
- **subject**: Change the job title (imperative form, e.g., "Run tests")
- **description**: Change the job description
- **activeForm**: Present continuous form shown in spinner when in_progress (e.g., "Running tests")
- **owner**: Change the job owner identifier. When a job is auto-linked to a `task`, this is typically the execution task ID (e.g. `task-1`).
- **metadata**: Merge metadata keys into the job (set a key to null to delete it)
- **addBlocks**: Mark jobs that cannot start until this one completes
- **addBlockedBy**: Mark jobs that must complete before this one can start
- **removeBlocks**: Remove jobs from this job's blocks list
- **removeBlockedBy**: Remove jobs from this job's blocked-by list

## Status Workflow
Status progresses: `pending` → `in_progress` → `completed`
A job can also move to `failed` when a linked task fails, then back to `in_progress` for retry.
Use `deleted` to permanently remove a job.

## Staleness
Make sure to read a job's latest state using `job_get` before updating it.

## Examples
Mark job as in progress when starting work:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "in_progress"}
```
Mark job as completed after finishing work:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "completed"}
```
Delete a job:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "deleted"}
```
Claim a job by setting owner:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "owner": "task-1"}
```
Set up job dependencies:
```json
{"jobId": "2", "addBlockedBy": ["1"]}
```
Remove a dependency after recovering:
```json
{"jobId": "2", "removeBlockedBy": ["1"]}
```

toolCalls.description:
Replaces text within a file. Use this tool for targeted edits to existing files.

The tool replaces exactly ONE occurrence of `old_string` with `new_string` by default. The `old_string` MUST uniquely identify the specific instance to change — unless `replace_all` is set to true, in which case every occurrence of `old_string` will be replaced. Use the read_file tool to establish the current file state before the first text replacement. If the tool reports that the file changed since it was read, re-read it before retrying.

If you need to replace the full contents of an existing file, use `write_file` instead.
If `old_string` is empty, the tool treats the operation as creating a new file. This is only valid when the target file does not already exist.
Do not use an empty `old_string` to overwrite an existing file.

The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.

Expectation for required parameters:
1. `file_path` MUST be an absolute path; otherwise an error will be thrown.
2. Prefer exact literal text for `old_string`, including relevant surrounding context and matching whitespace, indentation, and newlines when possible.
3. Prefer exact literal text for `new_string`, and ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic.
4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.

**Important:** Exact literal matches are the most reliable. The tool may also apply limited deterministic normalization for whitespace, indentation, and escaping, but do not rely on fallback matching when you can provide the exact text. The edit will FAIL if `old_string` is not unique in the file. Either provide a larger string with more surrounding context to make it unique or use `replace_all` to change every instance of `old_string`.
Use `replace_all` for replacing and renaming strings across the file. This parameter is useful if you want to rename a variable for instance.

messages.content: Maintenant ajouter les nouvelles méthodes admin à db.py :

toolCalls.name: replace

args.new_string:
def log_prediction(self, user_id: int, input_json: Dict[str, Any], prediction: str, probability: float, decision: str, explanation: Dict[str, Any] = None, client_id: int = None):
        """Log prediction (alias for create_prediction)."""
        self.create_prediction(user_id, input_json, prediction, probability, decision, explanation or {}, client_id)

    # ==================== ADMIN METHODS ====================

    def get_admin_stats(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get global platform statistics for admin dashboard."""
        with self.session() as s:
            total_users = s.execute(select(func.count(User.id))).scalar_one() or 0
            total_clients = s.execute(select(func.count(Client.id))).scalar_one() or 0
            total_predictions = s.execute(select(func.count(Prediction.id))).scalar_one() or 0
            active_subscriptions = s.execute(
                select(func.count(User.id)).filter(User.subscription_status == "active")
            ).scalar_one() or 0
            high_risk_cases = s.execute(
                select(func.count(Prediction.id)).filter(Prediction.prediction == "high_risk")
            ).scalar_one() or 0
            # Plan distribution
            plan_dist = s.execute(
                select(User.plan_tier, func.count(User.id)).group_by(User.plan_tier)
            ).all()
            # Role distribution
            admin_count = s.execute(
                select(func.count(User.id)).filter(User.role == "admin")
            ).scalar_one() or 0
            client_count = s.execute(
                select(func.count(User.id)).filter(User.role == "client")
            ).scalar_one() or 0
            # Active vs inactive
            active_count = s.execute(
                select(func.count(User.id)).filter(User.is_active == True)
            ).scalar_one() or 0
            inactive_count = s.execute(
                select(func.count(User.id)).filter(User.is_active == False)
            ).scalar_one() or 0
            # Recent predictions (last 7 days)
            from datetime import timedelta
            week_ago = datetime.utcnow() - timedelta(days=7)
            recent_predictions = s.execute(
                select(func.count(Prediction.id)).filter(Prediction.created_at >= week_ago)
            ).scalar_one() or 0
            # Monthly predictions for chart
            dialect = s.bind.dialect.name
            month_expr = func.strftime('%Y-%m', Prediction.created_at) if dialect == "sqlite" else func.to_char(Prediction.created_at, 'YYYY-MM')
            monthly = s.execute(
                select(month_expr, func.count(Prediction.id)).group_by(month_expr).order_by(month_expr)
            ).all()
            return {
                "total_users": total_users,
                "total_clients": total_clients,
                "total_predictions": total_predictions,
                "active_subscriptions": active_subscriptions,
                "high_risk_cases": high_risk_cases,
                "plan_distribution": {r[0]: int(r[1]) for r in plan_dist},
                "role_distribution": {"admin": admin_count, "client": client_count},
                "active_distribution": {"active": active_count, "inactive": inactive_count},
                "recent_predictions_week": recent_predictions,
                "monthly_predictions": [{"month": r[0], "count": int(r[1])} for r in monthly],
            }

    def get_all_predictions(self, limit: int = 100) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Get all predictions across all users (admin only)."""
        with self.session() as s:
            rows = s.execute(
                select(Prediction).order_by(Prediction.created_at.desc()).limit(limit)
            ).scalars().all()
            result = []
            for p in rows:
                pred_dict = {
                    "id": p.id,
                    "user_id": p.user_id,
                    "client_id": p.client_id,
                    "prediction": p.prediction,
                    "probability": p.probability,
                    "decision": p.decision,
                    "created_at": p.created_at.isoformat(),
                }
                # Enrich with user email
                if p.user_id:
                    user = s.get(User, p.user_id)
                    if user:
                        pred_dict["user_email"] = user.email
                result.append(pred_dict)
            return result

    def toggle_user_active(self, user_id: int) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Toggle user active/inactive status."""
        with self.session() as s:
            user = s.get(User, user_id)
            if not user:
                return None
            user.is_active = not user.is_active
            s.commit()
            return {
                "id": user.id,
                "email": user.email,
                "is_active": user.is_active,
            }

    def get_system_logs(self, limit: int = 50) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Get system logs for admin dashboard."""
        with self.session() as s:
            rows = s.execute(
                select(Log).order_by(Log.created_at.desc()).limit(limit)
            ).scalars().all()
            return [
                {
                    "id": l.id,
                    "action": l.action,
                    "level": l.level,
                    "details": l.details,
                    "created_at": l.created_at.isoformat(),
                }
                for l in rows
            ]

    def get_user_predictions(self, user_id: int, limit: int = 100) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
        """Get predictions for a specific user (admin viewing client data)."""
        with self.session() as s:
            rows = s.execute(
                select(Prediction)
                .filter_by(user_id=user_id)
                .order_by(Prediction.created_at.desc())
                .limit(limit)
            ).scalars().all()
            return [
                {
                    "id": p.id,
                    "user_id": p.user_id,
                    "client_id": p.client_id,
                    "prediction": p.prediction,
                    "probability": p.probability,
                    "decision": p.decision,
                    "created_at": p.created_at.isoformat(),
                }
                for p in rows
            ]

    def get_user_analytics(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Get analytics for a specific user (client's own data)."""
        with self.session() as s:
            total = s.execute(
                select(func.count(Prediction.id)).filter_by(user_id=user_id)
            ).scalar_one() or 0
            avg = s.execute(
                select(func.avg(Prediction.probability)).filter_by(user_id=user_id)
            ).scalar_one() or 0.0
            dist = s.execute(
                select(Prediction.prediction, func.count(Prediction.id))
                .filter_by(user_id=user_id)
                .group_by(Prediction.prediction)
            ).all()
            dialect = s.bind.dialect.name
            month_expr = func.strftime('%Y-%m', Prediction.created_at) if dialect == "sqlite" else func.to_char(Prediction.created_at, 'YYYY-MM')
            monthly = s.execute(
                select(month_expr, func.count(Prediction.id))
                .filter_by(user_id=user_id)
                .group_by(month_expr)
                .order_by(month_expr)
            ).all()
            return {
                "total_predictions": int(total),
                "average_probability": float(avg),
                "risk_distribution": {r[0]: int(r[1]) for r in dist},
                "monthly_predictions": [{"month": r[0], "count": int(r[1])} for r in monthly],
            }

toolCalls.description:
Use this tool to update a job in the job list.

## When to Use This Tool
**Mark jobs as resolved:**
- When you have completed the work described in a job
- When a job is no longer needed or has been superseded
- IMPORTANT: Always mark your assigned jobs as resolved when you finish them
- After resolving, call job_list to find your next job
- ONLY mark a job as completed when you have FULLY accomplished it
- If you encounter errors, blockers, or cannot finish, keep the job as in_progress
- When blocked, create a new job describing what needs to be resolved
- Never mark a job as completed if:
  - Tests are failing
  - Implementation is partial
  - You encountered unresolved errors
  - You couldn't find necessary files or dependencies

**Delete jobs:**
- When a job is no longer relevant or was created in error
- Setting status to `deleted` permanently removes the job

**Update job details:**
- When requirements change or become clearer
- When establishing dependencies between jobs

## Fields You Can Update
- **status**: The job status (see Status Workflow below)
- **subject**: Change the job title (imperative form, e.g., "Run tests")
- **description**: Change the job description
- **activeForm**: Present continuous form shown in spinner when in_progress (e.g., "Running tests")
- **owner**: Change the job owner identifier. When a job is auto-linked to a `task`, this is typically the execution task ID (e.g. `task-1`).
- **metadata**: Merge metadata keys into the job (set a key to null to delete it)
- **addBlocks**: Mark jobs that cannot start until this one completes
- **addBlockedBy**: Mark jobs that must complete before this one can start
- **removeBlocks**: Remove jobs from this job's blocks list
- **removeBlockedBy**: Remove jobs from this job's blocked-by list

## Status Workflow
Status progresses: `pending` → `in_progress` → `completed`
A job can also move to `failed` when a linked task fails, then back to `in_progress` for retry.
Use `deleted` to permanently remove a job.

## Staleness
Make sure to read a job's latest state using `job_get` before updating it.

## Examples
Mark job as in progress when starting work:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "in_progress"}
```
Mark job as completed after finishing work:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "completed"}
```
Delete a job:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "deleted"}
```
Claim a job by setting owner:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "owner": "task-1"}
```
Set up job dependencies:
```json
{"jobId": "2", "addBlockedBy": ["1"]}
```
Remove a dependency after recovering:
```json
{"jobId": "2", "removeBlockedBy": ["1"]}
```

messages.content:
### Phase 2 : Free plan — 3 prédictions gratuites

**1. Changer `check_prediction_limit()` dans db.py** — free = 3 prédictions lifetime :

toolCalls.name: replace

args.new_string:
def check_prediction_limit(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Check if user has reached their prediction limit.
        Free plan: 3 predictions lifetime.
        Pro/Enterprise: unlimited.
        """
        with self.session() as s:
            user = s.get(User, user_id)
            if not user:
                return {"allowed": True, "remaining": None, "limit_reached": False, "used": 0, "limit": None}
            
            # Pro and above have unlimited predictions
            if user.plan_tier in ("pro", "enterprise", "unlimited"):
                return {"allowed": True, "remaining": None, "limit_reached": False, "used": user.monthly_predictions_used, "limit": None}
            
            # Free plan: 3 predictions lifetime
            limit = 3
            used = user.monthly_predictions_used
            remaining = max(0, limit - used)
            limit_reached = used >= limit
            
            return {
                "allowed": not limit_reached,
                "remaining": remaining,
                "limit_reached": limit_reached,
                "used": used,
                "limit": limit
            }
args.old_string:
def check_prediction_limit(self, user_id: int) -> Dict[str, Any]:
        """Check if user has reached their prediction limit for the month."""
        from datetime import datetime, timedelta
        with self.session() as s:
            user = s.get(User, user_id)
            if not user:
                return {"allowed": True, "remaining": None, "limit_reached": False}
            
            # Only free plan has limits
            if user.plan_tier != "free":
                return {"allowed": True, "remaining": None, "limit_reached": False}
            
            # Check if we need to reset the monthly counter (new month)
            now = datetime.utcnow()
            if user.predictions_month_reset is None or user.predictions_month_reset < now:
                user.monthly_predictions_used = 0
                user.predictions_month_reset = datetime(now.year, now.month, 1) + timedelta(days=32)
                user.predictions_month_reset = user.predictions_month_reset.replace(day=1)  # First day of next month
                s.commit()
            
            limit = 100
            remaining = max(0, limit - user.monthly_predictions_used)
            limit_reached = user.monthly_predictions_used >= limit
            
            return {
                "allowed": not limit_reached,
                "remaining": remaining,
                "limit_reached": limit_reached,
                "used": user.monthly_predictions_used,
                "limit": limit
            }

toolCalls.description:
Use this tool to update a job in the job list.

## When to Use This Tool
**Mark jobs as resolved:**
- When you have completed the work described in a job
- When a job is no longer needed or has been superseded
- IMPORTANT: Always mark your assigned jobs as resolved when you finish them
- After resolving, call job_list to find your next job
- ONLY mark a job as completed when you have FULLY accomplished it
- If you encounter errors, blockers, or cannot finish, keep the job as in_progress
- When blocked, create a new job describing what needs to be resolved
- Never mark a job as completed if:
  - Tests are failing
  - Implementation is partial
  - You encountered unresolved errors
  - You couldn't find necessary files or dependencies

**Delete jobs:**
- When a job is no longer relevant or was created in error
- Setting status to `deleted` permanently removes the job

**Update job details:**
- When requirements change or become clearer
- When establishing dependencies between jobs

## Fields You Can Update
- **status**: The job status (see Status Workflow below)
- **subject**: Change the job title (imperative form, e.g., "Run tests")
- **description**: Change the job description
- **activeForm**: Present continuous form shown in spinner when in_progress (e.g., "Running tests")
- **owner**: Change the job owner identifier. When a job is auto-linked to a `task`, this is typically the execution task ID (e.g. `task-1`).
- **metadata**: Merge metadata keys into the job (set a key to null to delete it)
- **addBlocks**: Mark jobs that cannot start until this one completes
- **addBlockedBy**: Mark jobs that must complete before this one can start
- **removeBlocks**: Remove jobs from this job's blocks list
- **removeBlockedBy**: Remove jobs from this job's blocked-by list

## Status Workflow
Status progresses: `pending` → `in_progress` → `completed`
A job can also move to `failed` when a linked task fails, then back to `in_progress` for retry.
Use `deleted` to permanently remove a job.

## Staleness
Make sure to read a job's latest state using `job_get` before updating it.

## Examples
Mark job as in progress when starting work:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "in_progress"}
```
Mark job as completed after finishing work:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "completed"}
```
Delete a job:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "deleted"}
```
Claim a job by setting owner:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "owner": "task-1"}
```
Set up job dependencies:
```json
{"jobId": "2", "addBlockedBy": ["1"]}
```
Remove a dependency after recovering:
```json
{"jobId": "2", "removeBlockedBy": ["1"]}
```

Replaces text within a file. Use this tool for targeted edits to existing files.

The tool replaces exactly ONE occurrence of `old_string` with `new_string` by default. The `old_string` MUST uniquely identify the specific instance to change — unless `replace_all` is set to true, in which case every occurrence of `old_string` will be replaced. Use the read_file tool to establish the current file state before the first text replacement. If the tool reports that the file changed since it was read, re-read it before retrying.

If you need to replace the full contents of an existing file, use `write_file` instead.
If `old_string` is empty, the tool treats the operation as creating a new file. This is only valid when the target file does not already exist.
Do not use an empty `old_string` to overwrite an existing file.

The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.

toolCalls.description:
Use this tool to update a job in the job list.

## When to Use This Tool
**Mark jobs as resolved:**
- When you have completed the work described in a job
- When a job is no longer needed or has been superseded
- IMPORTANT: Always mark your assigned jobs as resolved when you finish them
- After resolving, call job_list to find your next job
- ONLY mark a job as completed when you have FULLY accomplished it
- If you encounter errors, blockers, or cannot finish, keep the job as in_progress
- When blocked, create a new job describing what needs to be resolved
- Never mark a job as completed if:
  - Tests are failing
  - Implementation is partial
  - You encountered unresolved errors
  - You couldn't find necessary files or dependencies

**Delete jobs:**
- When a job is no longer relevant or was created in error
- Setting status to `deleted` permanently removes the job

**Update job details:**
- When requirements change or become clearer
- When establishing dependencies between jobs

## Fields You Can Update
- **status**: The job status (see Status Workflow below)
- **subject**: Change the job title (imperative form, e.g., "Run tests")
- **description**: Change the job description
- **activeForm**: Present continuous form shown in spinner when in_progress (e.g., "Running tests")
- **owner**: Change the job owner identifier. When a job is auto-linked to a `task`, this is typically the execution task ID (e.g. `task-1`).
- **metadata**: Merge metadata keys into the job (set a key to null to delete it)
- **addBlocks**: Mark jobs that cannot start until this one completes
- **addBlockedBy**: Mark jobs that must complete before this one can start
- **removeBlocks**: Remove jobs from this job's blocks list
- **removeBlockedBy**: Remove jobs from this job's blocked-by list

## Status Workflow
Status progresses: `pending` → `in_progress` → `completed`
A job can also move to `failed` when a linked task fails, then back to `in_progress` for retry.
Use `deleted` to permanently remove a job.

## Staleness
Make sure to read a job's latest state using `job_get` before updating it.

## Examples
Mark job as in progress when starting work:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "in_progress"}
```
Mark job as completed after finishing work:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "completed"}
```
Delete a job:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "deleted"}
```
Claim a job by setting owner:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "owner": "task-1"}
```
Set up job dependencies:
```json
{"jobId": "2", "addBlockedBy": ["1"]}
```
Remove a dependency after recovering:
```json
{"jobId": "2", "removeBlockedBy": ["1"]}
```

toolCalls.description:
Use this tool to update a job in the job list.

## When to Use This Tool
**Mark jobs as resolved:**
- When you have completed the work described in a job
- When a job is no longer needed or has been superseded
- IMPORTANT: Always mark your assigned jobs as resolved when you finish them
- After resolving, call job_list to find your next job
- ONLY mark a job as completed when you have FULLY accomplished it
- If you encounter errors, blockers, or cannot finish, keep the job as in_progress
- When blocked, create a new job describing what needs to be resolved
- Never mark a job as completed if:
  - Tests are failing
  - Implementation is partial
  - You encountered unresolved errors
  - You couldn't find necessary files or dependencies

**Delete jobs:**
- When a job is no longer relevant or was created in error
- Setting status to `deleted` permanently removes the job

**Update job details:**
- When requirements change or become clearer
- When establishing dependencies between jobs

## Fields You Can Update
- **status**: The job status (see Status Workflow below)
- **subject**: Change the job title (imperative form, e.g., "Run tests")
- **description**: Change the job description
- **activeForm**: Present continuous form shown in spinner when in_progress (e.g., "Running tests")
- **owner**: Change the job owner identifier. When a job is auto-linked to a `task`, this is typically the execution task ID (e.g. `task-1`).
- **metadata**: Merge metadata keys into the job (set a key to null to delete it)
- **addBlocks**: Mark jobs that cannot start until this one completes
- **addBlockedBy**: Mark jobs that must complete before this one can start
- **removeBlocks**: Remove jobs from this job's blocks list
- **removeBlockedBy**: Remove jobs from this job's blocked-by list

## Status Workflow
Status progresses: `pending` → `in_progress` → `completed`
A job can also move to `failed` when a linked task fails, then back to `in_progress` for retry.
Use `deleted` to permanently remove a job.

## Staleness
Make sure to read a job's latest state using `job_get` before updating it.

## Examples
Mark job as in progress when starting work:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "in_progress"}
```
Mark job as completed after finishing work:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "completed"}
```
Delete a job:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "status": "deleted"}
```
Claim a job by setting owner:
```json
{"jobId": "1", "owner": "task-1"}
```
Set up job dependencies:
```json
{"jobId": "2", "addBlockedBy": ["1"]}
```
Remove a dependency after recovering:
```json
{"jobId": "2", "removeBlockedBy": ["1"]}
```

toolCalls.description:
Replaces text within a file. Use this tool for targeted edits to existing files.

The tool replaces exactly ONE occurrence of `old_string` with `new_string` by default. The `old_string` MUST uniquely identify the specific instance to change — unless `replace_all` is set to true, in which case every occurrence of `old_string` will be replaced. Use the read_file tool to establish the current file state before the first text replacement. If the tool reports that the file changed since it was read, re-read it before retrying.

If you need to replace the full contents of an existing file, use `write_file` instead.
If `old_string` is empty, the tool treats the operation as creating a new file. This is only valid when the target file does not already exist.
Do not use an empty `old_string` to overwrite an existing file.

The user has the ability to modify the `new_string` content. If modified, this will be stated in the response.

Expectation for required parameters:
1. `file_path` MUST be an absolute path; otherwise an error will be thrown.
2. Prefer exact literal text for `old_string`, including relevant surrounding context and matching whitespace, indentation, and newlines when possible.
3. Prefer exact literal text for `new_string`, and ensure the resulting code is correct and idiomatic.
4. NEVER escape `old_string` or `new_string`, that would break the exact literal text requirement.

**Important:** Exact literal matches are the most reliable. The tool may also apply limited deterministic normalization for whitespace, indentation, and escaping, but do not rely on fallback matching when you can provide the exact text. The edit will FAIL if `old_string` is not unique in the file. Either provide a larger string with more surrounding context to make it unique or use `replace_all` to change every instance of `old_string`.
Use `replace_all` for replacing and renaming strings across the file. This parameter is useful if you want to rename a variable for instance.

Object.entries(stats.plan_distribution).map(([name, value]) => ({ name: name.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + name.slice(1), value, })) : [] const monthlyData = stats?.monthly_predictions || [] const totalUsers = stats?.total_users || 0 const activeSubs = stats?.active_subscriptions || 0 const totalPredictions = stats?.total_predictions || 0 // Pricing in Dinar Algérien const planPricing = [ { name: "Free", price: 0, users: stats?.plan_distribution?.free || 0 }, { name: "Pro", price: 2500, users: stats?.plan_distribution?.pro || 0 }, { name: "Enterprise", price: 0, users: stats?.plan_distribution?.enterprise || 0 }, ] const estimatedMonthlyRevenue = (stats?.plan_distribution?.pro || 0) * 2500 const estimatedYearlyRevenue = estimatedMonthlyRevenue * 12 return (
